notes.title: • FILTER BASED ON ACTI
notes.body:
• Filter based on Active Cell use shortcut

Sub FilterByActiveCell()
    Dim DataRange As Range
    Dim Target As Range
   
    ' Set Target as the cell you currently have selected
    Set Target = ActiveCell
    Set DataRange = Target.CurrentRegion
   
    ' Check if the cell is within a data range and not empty
    If Not Intersect(Target, DataRange) Is Nothing And Target.Value <> "" Then
        ' Field is calculated by the relative position of the active cell in the range
        DataRange.AutoFilter Field:=Target.Column - DataRange.Column + 1, _
                             Criteria1:=Target.Value
    Else
        MsgBox "Please select a cell within your data range.", vbExclamation
    End If
End Sub

notes.title: • JUST LIKE CTR
notes.body:
• Just like Ctrl + D but Only Formatting

Sub FillDownFormatting()

    Dim selectedRange As Range
    Dim topCell As Range
   
    ' Work only with the currently highlighted cells
    Set selectedRange = Selection
   
    ' Identify the top row of the selection to use as the "Source"
    Set topCell = selectedRange.Rows(1)
   
    ' Copy the formatting from the top cell/row and paste to the whole range
    topCell.Copy
    selectedRange.PasteSpecial Paste:=xlPasteFormats
   
    ' Clear the clipboard "marching ants"
    Application.CutCopyMode = False
End Sub

notes.title: • REPLICATE 🎉 DOUBLE
notes.body:
• Replicate 🎉 Double Click for Autofill in Excel

Sub SatisfyingAutoFill()
    Dim lastRow As Long
    Dim sourceCell As Range
    Dim startRow As Long, endRow As Long
    Dim i As Integer
    Dim chunkSize As Long
 
    Set sourceCell = ActiveCell
 
    ' Logic Change: Look DOWN from the current row in the left column
    ' This stops at the first blank cell it encounters.
    lastRow = Cells(sourceCell.Row, sourceCell.Column - 1).End(xlDown).Row
 
    ' Safety check: If the adjacent column is empty or row is huge, don't run
    If lastRow > Rows.Count Or lastRow <= sourceCell.Row Then
        MsgBox "No contiguous data found in the left column.", vbExclamation
        Exit Sub
    End If
 
    ' Reset the visual animation logic
    chunkSize = (lastRow - sourceCell.Row) / 10
    If chunkSize < 1 Then chunkSize = 1
 
    startRow = sourceCell.Row
 
    For i = 1 To 10
        endRow = startRow + chunkSize
        If endRow > lastRow Or i = 10 Then endRow = lastRow
     
        Range(sourceCell, Cells(endRow, sourceCell.Column)).FillDown
     
        DoEvents
        Application.Wait (Now + TimeValue("0:00:01") / 10) ' Slightly faster pause
     
        startRow = endRow
    Next i
End Sub

notes.title: 😎 SECRET FOLDER IN WI
notes.body:
😎 Secret Folder in Windows

Vault Access: Harshit's CopyFlow
Use these commands in Command Prompt to toggle the visibility and system status of your folder.

🔒 Ghost Mode (Hide & Protect)
This doesn't just hide the folder; it marks it as a critical system file so it won't show up even if "Show Hidden Files" is turned on.

attrib +h +s "C:\Users\mlpit.SURYAMLPR\Documents\Harshit\CopyFlow"

+h → Sets the Hidden attribute.
+s → Sets the System attribute (super-stealth).

🔓 Reveal Mode (Unhide)
Bring your files back into the light with a quick reversal.

attrib -h -s "C:\Users\mlpit.SURYAMLPR\Documents\Harshit\CopyFlow"

-h → Removes the Hidden attribute.
-s → Removes the System attribute.

Notes

> The "Super Hidden" Trick: By using +s, the folder remains invisible even if someone checks "Hidden items" in File Explorer. They would have to uncheck "Hide protected operating system files" in settings to see it.
> Pro-tip: Stay organized. If you forget the path, you can't unhide it easily! Maybe keep a tiny text file note of your "vault" locations. 🗝️

notes.title: ➡️ CREATE FOLDER AND F
notes.body:
➡️ Create Folder and File in CMD 🐦‍🔥

○ type nul > filename.txt (Create an empty file) ○ echo Hello World > filename.txt (Create a file & add a line) ○ echo New line >> filename.txt (Add more line without removing the already existing data use two arrows ">>")


➡️ Run Commands
+------------------+----------------------+
| Command          | What it Opens        |
+==================+======================+
| msconfig         | System Configuration |
| services.msc     | Windows Services     |
| taskmgr          | Task Manager         |
| control          | Control Manager      |
| appwiz.cpl       | Programs & Features  |
| sysdm.cpl        | System Properties    |
| optionalFeatures | Windows Features     |
| powercfg.cpl     | Power Options        |
+------------------+----------------------+

+------------+--------------------------------+
| Command    | What it Opens                  |
+============+================================+
| cmd        | Command Prompt                 |
| powershell | PowerShell                     |
| regedit    | Registry Editor ⚠️             |
| gpedit.msc | Group Policy Editor (Pro Only) |
| eventvwr   | Event Viewer                   |
| perfmon    | Performance Monitor            |
| resmon     | Resource Monitor               |
+------------+--------------------------------+

+------------------+-------------------------+
| Command          | What it Opens           |
+==================+=========================+
| ncpa.cpl         | Network Connections     |
| inetcpl.cpl      | Internet Options        |
| firewall.cpl     | Windows Firewall        |
| mstsc            | Remote Desktop          |
| wf.msc           | Advanced Firewall       |
| cleanmgr         | Disk Cleanup            |
| control Printers | Devices & Printers      |
| msinfo32         | Full system information |
| dxdiag           | DirectX & graphics info |
| osk              | On-Screen Keyboard      |
| magnify          | Magnifier               |
+------------------+-------------------------+

notes.title: SCILAB LOBBY
notes.body:
SciLab Lobby

  1. Head to the Job BBS.
  2. Open your Sub Screen. If you have 50 Bug Fragments or something close to that, go ahead and read the Job BBS. If you do not, I recommend skipping this next Job until you have 50 Bug Fragments.
  3. Read "Please adopt a virus!" and accept the Job.
  4. Head to the Virus Lab.

Virus Lab

  1. Jack into the Computer.

SciLab Square

  1. Head to SciLab 2.

SciLab 2

  1. Head South-East after you go under the arch.
  2. Take the South-West path at the next split.
  3. Follow the path to the Job Requestor and a "Bunny" virus.
  4. Talk to the Job Requestor.
  5. Choose "Give" if you have 50 Bug Fragments. If not, get into a few virus battles in the area and collect your 50.
  6. You get "Bunny, TuffBunny, MegaBunny"!!
  7. Jack out.

Virus Lab

  1. Talk to the Man right next to the "Virus Breeder".
  2. Select "Please".
  3. You get the "Bunny R" Virus Chip!!

notes.title: 🗂️ TOOLFK IMPORTANT T
notes.body:
🗂️ Toolfk Important Tools

👉 Photo to Anime 
► https://www.toolfk.com/tools/ai-photo-to-anime.html

👉 Jigsaw Puzzle Maker
► https://www.toolfk.com/tools/online-jigsaw.html

👉 Photo to Manga
► https://www.toolfk.com/tools/ai-manga-generator.html

👉 Pixar Styled Photo
► Transform the character and scene from the uploaded image into the style of a Pixar animated movie. Emphasize a vibrant 3D computer-generated animation look, with highly detailed yet stylized textures for hair, skin, and clothing. The lighting should be cinematic and dynamic, creating depth and a sense of wonder. Characters should have expressive features typical of Pixar films, with exaggerated but appealing proportions. Ensure the overall aesthetic is bright, colorful, and embodies the whimsical, heartwarming quality found in Pixar's productions.

► https://www.toolfk.com/tools/ai-pixar-style.html

+-----+---+
|   SQL   |
+-----+---+
► SELECT *
  FROM inhabitant
  WHERE state = 'friendly'
  AND job LIKE '%smith';

--> means:

  • SELECT * → return all columns
  • FROM inhabitant → from the inhabitant table
  • WHERE → filter rows based on conditions

Conditions:

  1. state = 'friendly'
    • only include inhabitants whose state is "friendly"
  2. job LIKE '%smith'
    • LIKE is used for pattern matching
    • % means “any number of characters”
    • %smith means:
      • any text ending with "smith"
